jokes.text: He was. He must have had that reoccurring nightmare... where he misses a meal.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil woke up from sleep unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Will explains it as a nightmare about missing a meal - playing on Phil's appetite
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that establishes Uncle Phil's love of food as a running trait. Well-constructed with the 'nightmare' misdirection.

jokes.text: I'm watching a television program on farm practices in the Midwest.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks what Carlton is doing up on a school night
jokes.punchline: Carlton gives an overly earnest description of what's clearly a silly TV show
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton earnestness, trying to make watching TV sound educational and sophisticated.

jokes.text: Look, Arnold the pig is wearing Eddie Albert's pajamas.
jokes.setup: Carlton's supposedly watching an educational farm program
jokes.punchline: He's actually watching Green Acres, revealing the absurdity of his claim
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal that deflates Carlton's pretension. The specific Green Acres reference is both absurd and economical.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Uncle Phil, I thought I was alone.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks about a smell
jokes.punchline: Will assumes Phil is referring to bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Basic misdirection humor relying on bathroom comedy. Gets a reaction but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Smells like cheap cologne and fried chicken.
jokes.setup: There's an unidentified smell
jokes.punchline: Phil identifies it as Jazz's signature scent combination
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific detail that becomes a running gag. The combination is both specific and absurd.

jokes.text: I resent that. Oops.
jokes.setup: Jazz protests the cologne and fried chicken comment
jokes.punchline: His 'Oops' suggests he accidentally revealed himself
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reveal of Jazz's hiding spot through his own indignation.

jokes.text: Aunt Viv, can I borrow some money for protection while I'm in the slammer? I am kind of cute, you know.
jokes.setup: Will lands on jail in Monopoly
jokes.punchline: He asks for prison protection money, referencing his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that plays on prison stereotypes while maintaining Will's cockiness. Edgy for family sitcom.

jokes.text: Sorry, baby, the rent just went up.
jokes.setup: Will asks for money for 'protection'
jokes.punchline: Viv plays along with prison terminology while refusing
jokes.explanation: Clever continuation of the prison metaphor. Aunt Viv shows wit by playing along while declining.

jokes.text: I thought that only happened on The Cosby Show.
jokes.setup: Eric comments on the family spending time together
jokes.punchline: References another NBC sitcom known for family togetherness
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to network competition and sitcom tropes. Mildly clever but feels like easy pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Ain't his curfew he's worried about you violating.
jokes.setup: Eric mentions respecting curfew
jokes.punchline: Will implies Phil is worried about Eric violating Hilary
jokes.explanation: Standard overprotective father joke with sexual innuendo. Will's street-smart perspective vs. Eric's formality.

jokes.text: You must be Carlton. Your father really speaks highly of all of you at the law firm. No, my man. I'm Will. Who?
jokes.setup: Eric assumes Will is Carlton based on Phil's description
jokes.punchline: The mix-up reveals Phil doesn't talk about Will at work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's outsider status and Eric's assumptions based on class/appearance.

jokes.text: Man, I thought you sucked up. You're just a Dustbuster compared to that dude.
jokes.setup: Eric is being extremely complimentary
jokes.punchline: Will compares Carlton's sucking up to a weak vacuum cleaner vs Eric
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that's both original and perfectly captures the comparison. The Dustbuster reference is specific and visual.

jokes.text: Oh yeah? When I'm his age I'll be twice the suck-up.
jokes.setup: Will insults Carlton's sucking up abilities
jokes.punchline: Carlton takes it as a challenge and promises to suck up even more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - he completely misses that it's an insult and embraces it as a goal. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: She thinks I look like Blair Underwood without them.
jokes.setup: Eric mentions Hilary made him take off his glasses
jokes.punchline: He reveals Hilary's comparison to attractive actor Blair Underwood
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that establishes Eric's vanity and Hilary's poor judgment. Blair Underwood was a timely reference.

jokes.text: Obviously, Hilary's the one that needs the glasses.
jokes.setup: Eric claims to look like Blair Underwood
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Hilary needs glasses if she thinks that
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback that plays on the glasses setup. Economical and cuts right to the insult.

jokes.text: Close. It's Banks, Philip Banks. When I won the Markus v. State of California case.
jokes.setup: Carlton hopes for a James Bond film
jokes.punchline: Phil parodies 'Bond, James Bond' to introduce his legal victory video
jokes.explanation: Clever Bond parody that shows Phil's ego about his legal career. The format mimicry is well-executed.

jokes.text: Not without your banker.
jokes.setup: Viv says she needs to balance her checkbook
jokes.punchline: Phil makes a pun on 'Banks' being her banker
jokes.explanation: Dad joke level pun on the family name. Gets a groan more than a laugh but fits Phil's character.

jokes.text: I'd watch it with you but I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Phil asks who wants to watch his legal video
jokes.punchline: Will gives brutally honest response with fake politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will Smith delivery - starts like he's being polite then cuts to brutal honesty. Very quotable format.

jokes.text: So, Hilary, has Eric shown you his legal briefs?
jokes.setup: Eric is talking about law school and legal cases
jokes.punchline: Will makes double entendre about underwear/legal documents
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre. Not particularly creative but gets the expected reaction for innuendo.

jokes.text: I don't like that guy. He's a pompous, preppie, suck-up. He reminds me of someone.
jokes.setup: Will lists Eric's negative qualities
jokes.punchline: The implication is Eric reminds him of Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the obvious Carlton parallel. The 'reminds me of someone' creates the expectation perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, that reminds me, I need a back seat for my date tomorrow night.
jokes.setup: Discussion of treating women with respect
jokes.punchline: Will immediately pivots to needing a car backseat for sexual purposes
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that contrasts Will's priorities with Eric's respectfulness. Typical teenage male comedy.

jokes.text: Let's play Three Stooges. Hey, Moe!
jokes.setup: Alternative to rock-paper-scissors for car usage
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Three Stooges violence, then presumably hits Carlton
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy reference that plays on classic slapstick. The setup and execution work for the format.

jokes.text: Don't paper get nothing? Paper gets to ride his bike.
jokes.setup: Will loses at rock-paper-scissors and questions the rules
jokes.punchline: Carlton delivers the harsh reality about paper's 'prize'
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of Will's complaint. The bike riding as consolation prize is both mean and funny.

jokes.text: Not unless he's 'down with OPP.' 'Yeah, you know me.'
jokes.setup: Phil thinks he's having a heart attack from loud rap music
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv references current rap song, showing she knows the music
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Aunt Viv is hipper than expected. The Naughty by Nature reference was very current.

jokes.text: You've heard of the Batmobile, get a load of the Rapmobile.
jokes.setup: Will reveals his new car
jokes.punchline: He parodies the Batmobile name for his rap-themed vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good portmanteau that fits Will's style and sets up the car as a character element. Batmobile reference works.

jokes.text: I have never seen so much trash in one car.
jokes.setup: Family sees Will's heavily modified car
jokes.punchline: Viv's disgusted reaction to the car's condition
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction line. Gets the job done but isn't particularly creative or memorable.

jokes.text: Sorry, I didn't see you. As hard as that is to believe.
jokes.setup: Jazz apologizes for not seeing Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically notes his large size makes him hard to miss
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor from Phil about his weight. Good character moment that shows his awareness.

jokes.text: His mom used to date them.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Jazz's connection at Pep Boys auto parts
jokes.punchline: The connection is his mom dating the Pep Boys (the mascots)
jokes.explanation: Absurd and specific joke that takes the Pep Boys mascots literally. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, but I got this real fly Malcolm X air-freshener.
jokes.setup: Phil repeatedly asks about insurance
jokes.punchline: Will keeps mentioning car accessories instead, culminating in Malcolm X air freshener
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd detail that combines 90s car culture with civil rights iconography. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: The joke's on you, Mr. Banks. You can't throw me out... 'cause I'm already outside.
jokes.setup: Jazz is being threatened with ejection
jokes.punchline: He reveals he's already outside, preempting the typical Jazz throwing gag
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that subverts the running Jazz ejection gag. Shows awareness of the show's own patterns while being clever.

jokes.text: Okay, well, I'm Eric. How you doing?
jokes.setup: Will asks for an explanation about Eric's relationship with Hilary
jokes.punchline: Eric completely misunderstands and introduces himself to Will again
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Eric's social awkwardness. The misunderstanding feels natural and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Suddenly, I'm hungry for some fried chicken.
jokes.setup: Jazz appears in the scene
jokes.punchline: Will references the earlier 'cheap cologne and fried chicken' description
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reinforces the earlier joke about Jazz's signature scent. Good callback timing.

jokes.text: You already got her on the couch. Save your money.
jokes.setup: Eric wants to take Hilary out to dinner
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Eric has already achieved his romantic goals
jokes.explanation: Crude but economical joke about dating priorities. Fits Will's street-smart, sexually direct character.

jokes.text: What about my rap?
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions getting her wrap
jokes.punchline: Will plays on wrap/rap homophone, staying in character
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that fits Will's hip-hop persona. The pun works naturally in context.

jokes.text: Hilary's an ignorant somebody. She ain't even call me to tell me she quit me.
jokes.setup: Will is upset about Hilary dating Eric
jokes.punchline: He talks about their relationship like they were actually dating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inflated sense of his relationship with Hilary. The street vernacular fits.

jokes.text: That's what I'm saying. I'm a man, too. But Uncle Phil's an extremely bigger man.
jokes.setup: Will declares he's a man and deserves respect
jokes.punchline: Jazz agrees but notes Uncle Phil's superior size/authority
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that plays on both physical size and authority. Jazz's cowardly practicality is in character.

jokes.text: A wise butler sees nothing, hears nothing... and takes detailed notes for his book.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey witnesses Will preparing to disobey Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: He parodies 'see no evil' while revealing his own scheming plans
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line that shows his scheming nature and wit. The 'book' reference adds another layer. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Your piece of junk just backed into... the custom fender of a $35,000 automobile, pal.
jokes.setup: There's been a car accident
jokes.punchline: Eric reveals his car is extremely expensive, raising the stakes
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke about expensive car damage. Sets up the legal conflict but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: I got a whole living room full of soda cans.
jokes.setup: Eric mentions turning in soda cans to pay for damage
jokes.punchline: Will takes it literally and offers his recycling collection
jokes.explanation: Good literalism joke that fits Will's character. The visual of a living room full of cans is funny.

jokes.text: Lookit, man, this thing used to say 'cold chilling.' Now it says 'old chili.'
jokes.setup: Will's car was damaged in the accident
jokes.punchline: The damage changed his cool slogan to something about food
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows how the accident damaged Will's street cred. The transformation from cool to food is perfect.

jokes.text: What's the name of your insurance company? Yo' fault.
jokes.setup: Eric asks about Will's insurance company
jokes.punchline: Will gives the name as 'Yo' fault' - meaning it's Eric's fault
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake company name that sounds like an insurance company but actually assigns blame. Very quotable and clever.

jokes.text: I don't have none and I don't need none 'cause it's your fault.
jokes.setup: Will claims insurance through 'Yo' fault' company
jokes.punchline: He clarifies he has no insurance because he blames Eric
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to the insurance joke that explains Will's logic. Less clever than the setup but necessary.

jokes.text: I'll meet you upstairs, pooky-pooh.
jokes.setup: Phil and Viv are having a romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Viv uses an embarrassing pet name in front of Eric
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing pet name joke. Gets the expected cringe reaction but isn't particularly creative.

jokes.text: Court, huh? There's a ball and a hoop out back, we can go now, sucker.
jokes.setup: Eric says he'll see Will in court (legal)
jokes.punchline: Will interprets it as basketball court and challenges him
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay that shows Will's confidence and athleticism. The immediate challenge fits his competitive nature perfectly.

jokes.text: Now that's extremely creative, Carlton. 'Look behind you, there's Dad.'
jokes.setup: Carlton warns Will that their dad is approaching
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically praises Carlton's 'creative' warning method
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that shows Will's wit even in trouble. The mock praise format works well.

jokes.text: I sure hope Luther Vandross is in the house.
jokes.setup: Will sees the state marshal arriving
jokes.punchline: He hopes it's R&B singer Luther Vandross instead of legal trouble
jokes.explanation: Good wishful thinking joke with timely pop culture reference. Shows Will's musical knowledge and optimism.

jokes.text: I buy a car, I don't get insurance, I get in a wreck, and you get sued? That's right, Will. Man, works for me.
jokes.setup: Phil explains legal guardianship responsibility
jokes.punchline: Will finds this arrangement perfectly acceptable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will response showing his self-serving attitude. The casual acceptance of the unfair situation is very in character.

jokes.text: You know what, I don't think it's quite tight enough. Okay. But you can't blame me for my entrepreneurial spirit.
jokes.setup: Jazz is wearing a neck brace presumably for insurance fraud
jokes.punchline: He adjusts it tighter and justifies his scheming as business sense
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy combined with Jazz's opportunistic character. The 'entrepreneurial spirit' line is a nice touch.

jokes.text: When I realized I'd have to raise my right hand, I stopped to buy gloves. Could you just die?
jokes.setup: Court requires raising right hand to swear in
jokes.punchline: Hilary bought gloves because she's worried about her hand's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary vanity joke. Her priorities are completely skewed, focusing on appearance even in court. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I don't have to answer that.
jokes.setup: Eric greets everyone saying it's a nice day
jokes.punchline: Will treats the pleasantry like a legal interrogation
jokes.explanation: Good legal comedy that shows Will's defensive mindset. The deadpan delivery makes a simple line funnier.

jokes.text: I've seen him do a lot of chewing... but I ain't never seen him spit nothing out.
jokes.setup: Phil threatens to chew Eric up and spit him out
jokes.punchline: Will notes Phil's eating habits don't include spitting food out
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Phil's appetite that subverts the tough-guy metaphor perfectly. Highly quotable and brilliantly observational.

jokes.text: Home. Must be nice. My wife took mine in our divorce settlement. I hate her.
jokes.setup: Judge reads that the incident happened at the Banks' home
jokes.punchline: Judge reveals his bitter divorce and resentment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes the judge as bitter and unprofessional. The escalation from 'must be nice' to 'I hate her' works well.

jokes.text: I know, I just always wanted to say that.
jokes.setup: Will objects in court incorrectly
jokes.punchline: He admits he just wanted to use legal terminology he's seen on TV
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about legal TV shows. Will's honesty about his motivation is endearing and relatable. Very quotable.

jokes.text: My fault. A little too much CNN.
jokes.setup: Will uses inappropriate political language
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges he's been watching too much news
jokes.explanation: Self-aware follow-up that explains the political reference. Shows Will can recognize when he goes too far.

jokes.text: Honey flipped me the bird at a bus stop this morning. That's the most action I've had in six months.
jokes.setup: Will mentions getting flipped off by a woman
jokes.punchline: Judge reveals his pathetic romantic life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about loneliness that builds the judge's pathetic character. The escalation from insult to 'action' is good.

jokes.text: Yeah, keep hope alive. You are somebody.
jokes.setup: Will finishes his testimony
jokes.punchline: He ends with Jesse Jackson campaign slogans
jokes.explanation: Good political reference that shows Will's cultural awareness. The motivational slogans in court context is absurd.

jokes.text: Dude's got a gun, next thing you know I got six warning shots in my back.
jokes.setup: Jazz keeps his hands up in court
jokes.punchline: He references police brutality and 'warning shots in the back' contradiction
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary disguised as paranoid comedy. The 'warning shots in the back' oxymoron is clever and pointed.

jokes.text: Can't. They closed my account. They said I was allegedly writing bad checks. I don't know what they talking about. Those checks was perfectly legible.
jokes.setup: Judge asks Jazz for his account of events
jokes.punchline: Jazz misunderstands 'account' and confuses 'bad checks' with 'illegible'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double wordplay - 'account' as testimony vs bank account, and bad/illegible checks. Perfect Jazz logic and very quotable.

jokes.text: Because I don't know what they mean.
jokes.setup: Eric asks why Hilary wouldn't use his legal terminology
jokes.punchline: Hilary admits she doesn't understand the big words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - refreshingly honest about her intellectual limitations. The simplicity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oopsy.
jokes.setup: Phil reveals Eric should have been wearing glasses while driving
jokes.punchline: Hilary realizes she caused the accident by making Eric remove his glasses
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement for a major revelation. The casual 'oopsy' contrasts brilliantly with the legal implications.

jokes.text: Your J-ship, my number's in your file. Get with me sometime. Peace out.
jokes.setup: Will wins the court case
jokes.punchline: He inappropriately hits on the judge before leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will confidence moment. The casual pickup line to a judge is absurd and perfectly in character. 'Your J-ship' is clever.

jokes.text: Rock breaks your scissors, Carlton. Wait, hold it.
jokes.setup: They're playing rock-paper-scissors again at the end
jokes.punchline: The game continues with Will about to object again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that bookends the episode. Sets up the cyclical nature of their disputes over the car.
